meta.name: GAD-7 Generalized Anxiety Disorder Scale
meta.description: The GAD-7 is a concise and efficient self-report screening tool for anxiety symptoms, consisting of 7 items used to assess the severity of generalized anxiety symptoms over the past two weeks. It can also be used to screen for panic disorder, social anxiety, and PTSD.
meta.author: Spitzer, Kroenke, Williams & Löwe (2006)
meta.reference: Spitzer RL, Kroenke K, Williams JBW, Löwe B. A brief measure for assessing generalized anxiety disorder: the GAD-7. Arch Intern Med. 2006;166(10):1092-1097.
meta.instruction: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems?

questions.text: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying
Worrying too much about different things
Being so restless that it's hard to sit still
Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your anxiety score is within the normal range, suggesting no clinically significant anxiety symptoms over the past two weeks. Your current level of worry and nervousness falls within healthy limits.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current well-being through regular exercise and healthy stress management habits
Practice preventive relaxation activities such as deep breathing, meditation, or spending time in nature
Keep up a regular routine that includes adequate sleep, balanced nutrition, and enjoyable activities

ranges.label: Mild Anxiety
ranges.description: You may be experiencing mild anxiety symptoms such as excessive worry or nervousness that could slightly interfere with your daily comfort and concentration. These symptoms often respond well to self-help strategies.
suggestions: Learn and regularly practice relaxation techniques such as progressive muscle relaxation or mindfulness meditation
Maintain consistent physical exercise, which is one of the most effective natural anxiety reducers
Reduce caffeine and stimulant intake, as these can amplify anxiety symptoms significantly
Try keeping a worry journal to identify triggers and practice challenging irrational thoughts

ranges.label: Moderate Anxiety
ranges.description: Your anxiety symptoms are at a moderate level and are likely noticeably affecting your work performance, social interactions, and overall daily functioning. Professional evaluation is recommended at this stage.
suggestions: Consider consulting a therapist, counselor, or psychiatrist for professional evaluation and treatment planning
Cognitive behavioral therapy has strong research support as an effective treatment for generalized anxiety
Incorporate daily relaxation or mindfulness exercises into your routine to help manage persistent worry
Maintain regular sleep and exercise habits, as disruptions in these areas can significantly worsen anxiety
Avoid excessive use of alcohol, sedatives, or sleep medications as strategies for managing anxiety symptoms

ranges.label: Severe Anxiety
ranges.description: Your anxiety symptoms are at a severe level and are likely significantly impairing your ability to function in daily life, work, and relationships. Prompt professional evaluation and treatment are strongly recommended.
suggestions: Seeking evaluation from a psychiatrist or mental health professional is strongly recommended at this severity level
A combination of medication and psychotherapy such as CBT is often the most effective approach for severe anxiety
Build and maintain a reliable support system of people who understand your situation and can offer help
Learn to recognize the physical signs of panic attacks and practice grounding techniques to manage them
Work with a professional to gradually face anxiety-provoking situations rather than avoiding them
